meaning: to meet (up); to be seeing someone

expressions.english: to run into someone
How did you meet each other?
Let's stop seeing each other.
expressions.notes:
우리 = we; us
그만 -이/어/아요. = Stop + V-ing.; Now, let's just + V.

expressions.english: Do you want to meet up and talk?
expressions.notes: 애기하다 = to talk, speak, say, tell; to have a conversation; to tell a story

expressions.english: When shall we meet?
I have not met him/her yet.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄴ- 적 있다 = to have never p.p.

expressions.english: Have you ever met him/her in person?
expressions.notes:
직접 = in person, directly
-(으)ㄴ- 적 있다 = to have + p.p.

expressions.english: What are you going to do when you meet him/her?
We started to fight as soon as we met.
Where do you want to meet up?
I shouldn't have met him/her.
expressions.notes: -지 말았어야 했다 = should have not + p.p.

expressions.english: It was too late, so we couldn't see each other.
Is there anyone you are seeing lately?
expressions.notes: 있다 = to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.korean: 친구들 못 만난 지 오래 됐어요.
expressions.english: It's been a long time since I have seen my friends.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄴ- 지 오래 되다 = to have been a long time since

expressions.english: Decide when to meet and let me know.
expressions.notes:
시간 = time; hour
정하다 = to decide
알려주다 = to let someone know; to teach something to someone

expressions.english: Whenever we meet up, we go to a coffee shop.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄴ- 때마다 = whenever + S + V
커피숍 = coffee shop
가다 = to go; to leave

expressions.korean: 입단 만나 보면 생각이 바뀔 거예요.
expressions.english: Once you meet him/her, you will change your mind.
expressions.notes:
입단 = once (+ S + V); first of all
-이/어/어 보다 = to try V-ing
입단 -이/어/어 보면 = once + S + V, (S + will/might + V₂)
생각 = thought; opinion; idea
바뀌다 = to change; to be changed

expressions.english: Yesterday, I saw a friend on the street by chance.
Everyone I met laughed at me.
expressions.notes:
-마다 = every + N
웃다 = to laugh, smile

expressions.korean: 만날 수만 있다면 뭐든지 할 거예요.
expressions.english: As long as I can meet him/her, I will do whatever it takes.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄴ- 수만 있다면 = as long as + S + can + V
뭐든지 = whatever it is, anything
